summary: Ran combined PHP/static + isolated DOM regression tests for Nepal address data, KYC selectors, mobile menu drawer JS, and institutional profile field wiring. 6/7 automated checks passed; one backward-compatibility issue found in public institutional profile rendering.

critical.issue: Public profile page accesses new DB columns directly (e.g., $p['other_fund']) without null-safe fallback, which can trigger Undefined array key notices on older databases where those columns are not yet present.

integration_issues.issue: Workspace database is not configured; public URL serves setup page, so end-to-end DB-backed UI flows cannot be validated in this run.

action_items: Update /app/institutional-profile.php to use null-safe access for optional/new columns (other_fund, bank_cash_balance, fixed_assets, total_loan_members), e.g. ($p['field'] ?? 0) patterns.
After fix, rerun /app/tests/test_php_feature_regression.py and then retest with configured DB to validate real public/admin render flows.
critical_code_review_comments: Address dataset integrity checks are good: 7 provinces, 77 districts, 753 local levels, no duplicate municipality names within district scope.
Target correction entries verified: दोधारा चाँदनी नगरपालिका, पचालझरना गाउँपालिका, and दोर्दी गाउँपालिका wards=9.
Mobile drawer JS behavior validated in isolated DOM: open/close, chevron injection, submenu toggle, aria-expanded updates all work.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (isolated DOM test), E2E blocked by DB setup

test_credentials: Read /app/memory/test_credentials.md. No test credentials available in workspace (DB not configured).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Automated regression exists at /app/tests/test_php_feature_regression.py. Current sole failing check is null-safe rendering expectation in /app/institutional-profile.php. Mobile JS and address dataset checks are already passing.
rca of the issue: RCA: New institutional profile fields were added in admin schema/forms, but public renderer still assumes all keys always exist in fetched row arrays. On environments where migration/ALTER hasn't run (or partial schema), direct access can emit Undefined array key notices and unstable rendering. Mitigation: null-coalescing defaults and defensive casting before condition/render logic.
